request.description:
# Retail Store Energy Rating Calculator

## Overview

This POST request calculates the NABERS Energy rating for a Retail Store based on the provided parameters. The calculation takes into account various factors such as gross floor area, student numbers, energy consumption data, and renewable energy information.

## Request Method and URL

- **Method**: POST
    
- **URL**: [https://data.nabers.gov.au/ratingcalculator/calculate](https://data.nabers.gov.au/ratingcalculator/calculate)
    

## Headers

| Header | Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key | {{vault:API_KEY}} | API subscription key required for authentication |

## Required Headers

| Header Name | Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key | {{vault:API_KEY}} | API subscription key for authentication |

## Request Body

The request body is a JSON object containing two main sections:

1. Control Object - Contains basic information about the facility
    
2. RatingInputs Object - Contains facility characteristics data and detailed energy consumption
    

### Control Object

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| postcode | Number | Yes | Australian postcode of the retail store |
| premiseType | String | Yes | Type of premise (must be "Retail store") |
| ratingperiodFrom | String | Yes | Start date of the rating period in YYYY-MM-DD format |
| SiteName | String | Yes | Name of the retail store site |
| EmailAddress | String | Yes | Contact email address |
| CompanyName | String | Yes | Name of the company |
| BuildingAddress | String | Yes | Physical address of the retail store |

#### RatingInputs Object

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| isStandAlone | String | Yes | Whether the retail store is standalone ("Yes" or "No") |
| serviceArrangement | String | Yes | Service arrangement type (e.g., "Centrally Serviced") |
| totalArea | Number | Yes | Total area of the retail store in square meters |
| totalHours | Number | Yes | Total operating hours per week |
| retailCategory | String | Yes | Retail category code (e.g., "R1") |
| totalOREGConsumption | Number | Yes | Total on-site renewable electricity generation consumption in kWh |
| netOREGConsumption | Number | Yes | Net on-site renewable electricity generation consumption in kWh |
| totalOnsiteLGCsExported | Number | Yes | Total on-site Large-scale Generation Certificates exported |
| totalOffsitePurchases | Number | Yes | Total off-site renewable electricity purchases in kWh |
| totalElectricityInclusions | Number | Yes | Total electricity inclusions in kWh |
| totalElectricityExclusions | Number | Yes | Total electricity exclusions in kWh |
| totalGasAndLPGInclusions | Number | Yes | Total gas and LPG inclusions in MJ |
| totalGasAndLPGExclusions | Number | Yes | Total gas and LPG exclusions in MJ |
| totalGreenPowerConsumption | Number | Yes | Total GreenPower consumption in kWh |
| totalGreenPowerPurchasedSeperately | Number | Yes | Total GreenPower purchased separately in kWh |
| offsiteLGCsSurrendered | Number | Yes | Off-site Large-scale Generation Certificates surrendered |

#### EnergyInputs Object

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| electricity | Number | Electricity consumption in kWh |
| greenpower | Number | Greenpower consumption percentage |
| gas | Number | Gas consumption in MJ |
| lpg | Number | LPG consumption in MJ |
| diesel | Number | Diesel consumption in L (0 if not used) |

## Response

The API returns a JSON object with the following structure:

### Expected Response

``` json
[
  {
    "waterResults": null,
    "sequence": 1,
    "requestId": null,
    "lstExceptions": null,
    "ieResults": null,
    "energyResults": {
      "totalRenewableElectricity": 179420.51,
      "totalEnergyUse": 360217.046,
      "totalEmissionsScope12WithRenewableElectricity": 365666.09,
      "totalEmissionsScope123WithRenewableElectricity": 414687.34,

      "onsiteRenewableElectricity": 54212.97,
      "nonRenewableElectricityREIPercent": 29.8,
      "nonRenewableElectricity": 429684.57,
      "noGreenPowerStarRatingGranular": 3.2,
      "noGreenPowerStarRatingDecimal": 3.2504369857661035,
      "noGreenPowerStarRating": 3.0,
      "noGreenPowerPredictedGHGIntensity": 106.11695356470001,
      "noGreenPowerGHGIntensityScope123": 106.10458780516666,
      "noGreenPowerGHGIntensityScope12": 96.6889588935,
      "noGreenPowerGHGEmissionsScope123": 63662.7526831,
      "noGreenPowerGHGEmissionsScope12": 58013.3753361,
      "noGreenPowerBenchmarkingFactor": 0.9998834704623724,
      "greenPowerREIPercent": 4.0,
      "greenPower": 58027.92,
      "greenHouseGasIntensityScope12WithRenewableElectricity": 609.44,
      "greenHouseGasIntensityScope123WithRenewableElectricity": 691.15,
      "greenhouseGasIntensityScope123": 106.10458780516666,
      "greenhouseGasIntensityScope12": 96.6889588935,
      "greenhouseGasEmissionsScope123": 63662.7526831,
      "greenhouseGasEmissionsScope12": 58013.3753361,

      "dieselREIPercent": 0.0,

 ```

### Response Fields

| Parameter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| noGreenPowerStarRating | The NABERS star rating without GreenPower |

| energyIntensity | Energy intensity in MJ/m²/year |

| greenhouseGasIntensityScope123 | Greenhouse gas intensity (Scope 1, 2 & 3) in kg CO₂-e/m² |
| benchmarkingFactor | Factor used for benchmarking |
| totalEnergyUse | Total energy use in MJ |
| rEIResultPercent | Renewable Energy Index result as a percentage |

## Notes

- The rating period is typically 12 months
    
- The API requires a valid subscription key for authentication
    
- Different building types may require different input parameters
    

## References

This documentation is based on the NABERS Energy Rating Calculator API specifications.

## NABERS Energy Performance Indicator Calculator

## Overview

This POST request calculates the Energy Performance Indicator for a Childcare Centre based on the provided parameters. The calculation takes into account various factors such as total area, energy consumption data, and renewable energy information.

## Request Method and URL

- **Method**: POST

| Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key | {{vault:API_KEY}} | API subscription key for authentication |

The request body is a JSON object containing two main sections:

1. Control contains basic information about the building such as postcode, building type, scope etc.
    
2. RatingInputs contains the following:
    
    1. Building attributes such as totalArea

``` json
      "greenhouseGasIntensityScope12": 0.7189285714285715,
      "greenhouseGasEmissionsScope123": 85.39999999999999,
      "greenhouseGasEmissionsScope12": 80.52000000000001,
      "gasAndLPGREIPercent": 57.7,
      "energyIntensity": 3.9214285714285713,
      "emissionFactorElectricity": 0.66,
      "dieselREIPercent": 0,
      "calculatedPUE": null,
      "benchmarkingFactor": null,
      "actualEmissionsWoGP": null,
      "actualEmissions": null
    }
  }
]

 ```

## Response Fields

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  |
| sequence | Number | Sequence number of the calculation |
| requestId | Number | Request identifier |
| lstExceptions |  | List of exceptions if any |
| energyResults | Object | Energy rating calculation results |
| totalRenewableElectricity | Number | Total renewable electricity in kWh |
| totalEnergyUse | Number | Total energy use in MJ |
| totalEmissionsScope12WithRenewableElectricity | Number | Total emissions (Scope 1 & 2) with renewable electricity |
| totalEmissionsScope123WithRenewableElectricity | Number | Total emissions (Scope 1, 2 & 3) with renewable electricity |
| sectorComparisonDial | String | Sector comparison information (e.g., "Excellent") |
| rETAndStateTerritoryTargetsREIPercent | Number | Renewable energy target percentage |
| rETAndStateTerritoryTargets | Number | Renewable energy targets in kWh |
| rEIResultPercentDecimal | Number | Renewable energy index result as decimal |
| rEIResultPercent | Number | Renewable energy index result as percentage |
| performanceIndicatorResult | String | Performance indicator result (e.g., "Excellent - high") |
| otherVoluntaryPurchasesREIPercent | Number | Other voluntary purchases as percentage |
| otherVoluntaryPurchases | Number | Other voluntary purchases in kWh |
| onsiteRenewableElectricityREIPercent | Number | Onsite renewable electricity percentage |
| onsiteRenewableElectricity | Number | Onsite renewable electricity in kWh |
| nonRenewableElectricityREIPercent | Number | Non-renewable electricity percentage |
| nonRenewableElectricity | Number | Non-renewable electricity in kWh |
| noGreenPowerStarRating | Null | Star rating without GreenPower |
| noGreenPowerGHGIntensityScope123 | Number | GHG intensity (Scope 1, 2 & 3) without GreenPower |
| noGreenPowerGHGIntensityScope12 | Number | GHG intensity (Scope 1 & 2) without GreenPower |
| noGreenPowerGHGEmissionsScope123 | Number | GHG emissions (Scope 1, 2 & 3) without GreenPower |
| noGreenPowerGHGEmissionsScope12 | Number | GHG emissions (Scope 1 & 2) without GreenPower |
| noGreenPowerBenchmarkingFactor | Number | Benchmarking factor without GreenPower |
| greenPowerREIPercent | Number | GreenPower as percentage of renewable energy index |
| greenpowerPercentage | Null | GreenPower percentage |
| greenPower | Number | GreenPower in kWh |
| greenHousGasEmissionDiesel | Null | Greenhouse gas emissions from diesel |
| greenHouseGasIntensityScope12WithRenewableElectricity | Number | Greenhouse gas intensity (Scope 1 & 2) with renewable electricity |
| greenHouseGasIntensityScope123WithRenewableElectricity | Number | Greenhouse gas intensity (Scope 1, 2 & 3) with renewable electricity |
| greenhouseGasIntensityScope123 | Number | Greenhouse gas intensity (Scope 1, 2 & 3) |
| greenhouseGasIntensityScope12 | Number | Greenhouse gas intensity (Scope 1 & 2) |
| greenhouseGasEmissionsScope123 | Number | Greenhouse gas emissions (Scope 1, 2 & 3) |
| greenhouseGasEmissionsScope12 | Number | Greenhouse gas emissions (Scope 1 & 2) |
| gasAndLPGREIPercent | Number | Gas and LPG as percentage of renewable energy index |
| energyIntensity | Number | Energy intensity |
| emissionFactorElectricity | Number | Emission factor for electricity |
| dieselREIPercent | Number | Diesel as percentage of renewable energy index |

## Notes

- The API requires a valid subscription key for authentication
    
- Different building types may require different input parameters
    
- This endpoint is specifically for Childcare Centre Energy Performance Indicator calculations
    

## References

This documentation is based on the NABERS Energy Rating Calculator API specifications.

item.description:
## What is the Rating Calculator API ? The NABERS Rating Calculator API allows the customer to programmatically calculate NABERS rating data directly into their own built applications. The NABERS Rating Calculator API is an API that the customer can call to retrieve the NABERS algorithm based on specific parameters that will be included in each call. The Rating Calculator API is available for the following sectors, scopes and rating types | **Premise type** | **Building Scope** | **Subsector** | **Rating Type** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Office | Base Building
Whole Building
Tenancy | | Energy
Water
Inddor Environment | | ShoppingCentre | Not applicable | | Energy
Water | | Hotel | Not applicable | | Energy
Water | | DataCentre | Infrastructure
IT equipment
Whole Facility | | Energy | | ApartmentBuilding | Not applicable | | Energy
Water | | RACRL | Residential Aged Care
Retirement Living
Co-Located | | Energy
Water | | Warehouse and cold store | Not applicable | | Energy | | School | Not applicable | Government
Independent
Catholic | Energy
Water | | Retail store | Not applicable | | Energy | | Energy Performance Indicator ** (For the values to send to rating Calculator please see the individual values in the table "Energy Performance Indicator Premise Type below) | | | Energy | ## Bulk Requests The Rating calculator supports bulks requests in a single call to the calculator. This feature can be useful for customers who need to make multiple calls to the calculator based on different input parameters. For example, a customer may like to send three different requests for an office building where the computer count, area and consumption inputs differ in each request. The calculator would then return three different output with different calculation for every request sent. The recommended number of requests in a single call is 100 for optimal performance. An example of a bulk call is provided in the folder, it is labelled "Bulk Rating calculator request example for Offices" ## API Call Limits Currently there are both daily and minute limits than can be made to the Rating Register API per subscription. These are indicated in the table below. | **Type of Limit** | **Amount** | | --- | --- | | Daily limit | 1000 calls per day | | Calls per minute | 200 calls per minute | | Bulk request | 100 calculations in a single request | ## Real-time Data The API calls directly the calculator API and results are returned in real-time ## Potential Errors Below is a list of some errors you might receive when calling the Rating Calculator API | **Error Code** | **Error Message** | **Possible reasons** | | --- | --- | --- | | 401 | Access denied due to invalid subscription key. Make sure to provide a valid key for an active subscription. | Please check that a valid API subscription key is entered in the header of the request. | | 404 | Resource not found | Please check the URL syntax for errors | | | The climate zone for this postcode 27500 cannot be found. Please contact NABERS for support.
The gross emission factor for this postcode 27500 cannot be found. Please contact NABERS for support.
The NGA emission factor for this postcode 27500 cannot be found. Please contact NABERS for support. | The postCode used in the request is invalid. Please verify the value entered. | | N/A | Invalid premiseType value | The entered premiseType is wrong. Please verify the value entered. You can reference the sample request to find the right syntax | | N/A | Invalid ratingPeriodFrom value | The entered ratingPeriodFrom is wrong. Please verify the value entered. You can reference the sample request to find the right syntax | | N/A | Invalid ratingScope value | The entered ratingScope is wrong. Please verify the value entered. You can reference the sample request to find the right syntax | | N/A | Malformed JSON: Expected '{' at the beginning of object | If the request structure is wrong then this message is shown. Check the input request and make sure it has all rating input parameters like control, ratinginputs and waterinputs and the values entered against each input request variable is correct. You can reference the sample request to find the right syntax | The sample requests in this folder are meant to guide our customers on how to structure their API calls. If you are still facing issue using the Rating Calculator API then please reach out to NABERS team.

item.name: Reverse calculator

request.description:
# Office Base Building Energy Reverse Rating Calculator

## Overview

This endpoint uses the NABERS reverse calculator to determine the energy consumption required to achieve a specific NABERS star rating for an Office Base Building. The reverse calculator works by taking a target star rating and building characteristics as inputs, then calculating the required energy consumption to achieve that rating.

### Key Information

- **Purpose**: Calculate the energy consumption required to achieve a specific NABERS star rating
    
- **Authentication**: Requires an API subscription key in the header
    
- **Request Format**: JSON array containing building details and target rating
    
- **Response**: Returns the calculated energy consumption values needed to achieve the target rating
    

---

## Request Method and URL

- **Method:** POST
    
- **URL:** `https://data.nabers.gov.au/reversecalculator/calculate`
    

## Headers

| Key | Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key | `****` (masked) | API subscription key (required) |
| Authorization | Bearer | Bearer token for authentication (required) |

## Request Body

The request body is a JSON object containing a few sections:

1. Fields with basic information such as target star rating, postcode, premise type and rating type
    
2. premiseInputs object containing information about the premise attributes.
    
3. enrConsumptionInputs containing information about consumption for electricity, gas and diesel
    

### 1 Input Fields

| Field | Type | Description | Example Value |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| targetStar | string | Target NABERS star rating (enum) | "5.7" |
| premiseType | string | Type of premise (enum) | "Office" |
| ratingScope | string | Scope of rating | "Base Building" |
| ratingType | string | Type of rating | "Energy" |
| premiseInputs | object | Inputs related to the premise | See nested table below |
| enrConsumptionInputs | object | Energy consumption inputs | See nested table below |

### 2 premiseInputs Object

| Field | Type | Description | Example Value |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| postcode | string | Postcode of the premise | "2615" |
| numberOfComputers | string | Number of computers (decimal) | "0.000000" |
| netLettableArea | string | Net lettable area in square meters | "10888.00" |
| hours | string | Operating hours per week | "40" |

### 3 enrConsumptionInputs Object

| Field | Type | Description | Example Value |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| electricityPercentage | string | Percentage of electricity usage | "90" |
| gasPercentage | string | Percentage of gas usage | "7" |
| dieselPercentage | string | Percentage of diesel usage | "3" |

---

## Expected Response

``` json
[
  {
    "targetStar": "5.7",
    "premiseType": "Office",
    "ratingScope": "Base Building",
    "ratingType": "Energy",
    "premiseInputs": {
      "postcode": "2615",
      "numberOfComputers": "0.000000",
      "netLettableArea": "10888.00",
      "hours": "40"
    },
    "enrConsumptionInputs": {
      "electricityPercentage": "90",
      "gasPercentage": "7",
      "dieselPercentage": "3"
    },
    "energyResults": {
      "energyConsumption": "1,151,000.00",
      "energyConsumptionPerM2": "105.71",
      "energyConsumptionPerM2Benchmark": "105.71",
      "energyConsumptionPerM2BenchmarkNormalized": "105.71",
      "energyConsumptionPerM2Normalized": "105.71",
      "energyConsumptionNormalized": "1,151,000.00",
      "starRating": "5.7",
      "starRatingNormalized": "5.7"
    },
    "fuelConsumption": {
      "electricity": "1,035,900.00",
      "gas": "80,570.00",
      "diesel": "34,530.00"
    },
    "emissions": {
      "emissionsConsumption": "1,035,900.00",
      "emissionsConsumptionPerM2": "95.14",
      "emissionsConsumptionPerM2Benchmark": "95.14",
      "emissionsConsumptionPerM2BenchmarkNormalized": "95.14",
      "emissionsConsumptionPerM2Normalized": "95.14",
      "emissionsConsumptionNormalized": "1,035,900.00"
    }
  }
]

 ```

## energyResults Object

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| energyConsumption | string | Total energy consumption (MJ) |
| energyConsumptionPerM2 | string | Energy consumption per square meter (MJ/m²) |
| energyConsumptionPerM2Benchmark | string | Benchmark energy consumption per square meter (MJ/m²) |
| energyConsumptionPerM2BenchmarkNormalized | string | Normalized benchmark energy consumption per square meter (MJ/m²) |
| energyConsumptionPerM2Normalized | string | Normalized energy consumption per square meter (MJ/m²) |
| energyConsumptionNormalized | string | Normalized total energy consumption (MJ) |
| starRating | string | Calculated NABERS star rating |
| starRatingNormalized | string | Normalized NABERS star rating |

## fuelConsumption Object

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| electricity | string | Electricity consumption (MJ) |
| gas | string | Gas consumption (MJ) |
| diesel | string | Diesel consumption (MJ) |

## emissions Object

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| emissionsConsumption | string | Total emissions (kg CO₂-e) |
| emissionsConsumptionPerM2 | string | Emissions per square meter (kg CO₂-e/m²) |
| emissionsConsumptionPerM2Benchmark | string | Benchmark emissions per square meter (kg CO₂-e/m²) |
| emissionsConsumptionPerM2BenchmarkNormalized | string | Normalized benchmark emissions per square meter (kg CO₂-e/m²) |
| emissionsConsumptionPerM2Normalized | string | Normalized emissions per square meter (kg CO₂-e/m²) |
| emissionsConsumptionNormalized | string | Normalized total emissions (kg CO₂-e) |

## Notes

- The API requires a valid subscription key for authentication
    
- Different building types may require different input parameters
    
- Reverse calculators give you an indication of the maximum amount of energy or water your building can use to achieve your desired rating.
    

## References

This documentation is based on the NABERS Energy Rating Calculator API specifications.

# Office Whole Building Energy Reverse Rating Calculator

## Overview

This endpoint uses the NABERS reverse calculator to determine the energy consumption required to achieve a specific NABERS star rating for an Office Whole Building. The reverse calculator works by taking a target star rating and building characteristics as inputs, then calculating the required energy consumption to achieve that rating.

### Key Information

- **Purpose**: Calculate the energy consumption required to achieve a specific NABERS star rating
    
- **Authentication**: Requires an API subscription key in the header
    
- **Request Format**: JSON array containing building details and target rating
    
- **Response**: Returns the calculated energy consumption values needed to achieve the target rating
    

---

## Request Method and URL

- **Method:** POST
    
- **URL:** `https://data.nabers.gov.au/reversecalculator/calculate`
    

## Headers

| Key | Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key | `****` (masked) | API subscription key (required) |
| Authorization | Bearer | Bearer token for authentication (required) |

## Request Body

The request body is a JSON object containing a few sections:

1. Fields with basic information such as target star rating, postcode, premise type and rating type
    
2. premiseInputs object containing information about the premise attributes.
    
3. enrConsumptionInputs containing information about consumption for electricity, gas and diesel
    

### 1 Input Fields

| Field | Type | Description | Example |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| targetStar | string | Target star rating (enum) | "5.578" |
| premiseType | string | Type of premise (enum) | "Office" |
| ratingScope | string | Scope of rating | "Whole Building" |
| ratingType | string | Type of rating | "Energy" |
| premiseInputs | object | Inputs related to the premise | See nested table |
| enrConsumptionInputs | object | Energy consumption inputs | See nested table |

### 2 premiseInputs Object

| Field | Type | Description | Example |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| postcode | string | Postcode of the premise | "2000" |
| numberOfComputers | string | Number of computers | "513" |
| numberOfApartments | string or null | Number of apartments (nullable) | null |
| netLettableArea | string | Net lettable area in square meters | "610151" |
| hours | string | Operating hours per week | "48.5" |

### 3 enrConsumptionInputs Object

| Field | Type | Description | Example |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| lpgPercentage | string | Percentage of LPG consumption | "0" |
| gasPercentage | string | Percentage of gas consumption | "5.000000000000" |
| electricityPercentage | string | Percentage of electricity consumption | "91.000000000000" |
| dieselPercentage | string | Percentage of diesel consumption | "4" |

## Expected Response

``` json
[
  {
    "waterResults": null,
    "sequence": 1,
    "lstErrors": null,
    "energyResults": {
      "targetResidual": null,
      "targetMaxEnergyBF": null,
      "primaryIntermediateOutputs": null,
      "predictedNGE": "N/A",
      "predictedGE": 58.1029108772759736242188000200347,
      "fuelConsumption": {
        "targetMaxLPG": null,
        "targetMaxGas": 9650540,
        "targetMaxEnergyIntensity": 316,
        "targetMaxElec": 48788842,
        "targetMaxDiesel": 200011
      },
      "emissions": {
        "targetMaxEmissionsIntensity": 58.1029108772759736242188000200347,
        "targetMaxEmissions": 35451549.1746808125827907250510241922397,
        "predictedAveEmissions": null,
        "actualTargetScope1_2_3": 35451440,
        "actualTargetScope1_2": 33239901
      }
    }
  }
]

 ```

## energyResults Object

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| targetResidual | null/number | Target residual energy value |
| targetMaxEnergyBF | null/number | Target maximum energy before factors |
| primaryIntermediateOutputs | null/object | Intermediate calculation outputs |
| predictedNGE | string | Predicted net greenhouse emissions |
| predictedGE | number | Predicted greenhouse emissions |
| fuelConsumption | object | Breakdown of fuel consumption targets |
| emissions | object | Emissions-related calculations |

## fuelConsumption Object

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| targetMaxLPG | null/number | Target maximum LPG consumption |
| targetMaxGas | number | Target maximum gas consumption in MJ/year |
| targetMaxEnergyIntensity | number | Target maximum energy intensity in MJ/m²/year |
| targetMaxElec | number | Target maximum electricity consumption in MJ/year |
| targetMaxDiesel | number | Target maximum diesel consumption in MJ/year |

## emissions Object

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| targetMaxEmissionsIntensity | number | Target maximum emissions intensity in kg CO₂-e/m²/year |
| targetMaxEmissions | number | Target maximum emissions in kg CO₂-e/year |
| predictedAveEmissions | null/number | Predicted average emissions |
| actualTargetScope1_2_3 | number | Actual target emissions for scopes 1, 2, and 3 |
| actualTargetScope1_2 | number | Actual target emissions for scopes 1 and 2 |

## Notes

- The API requires a valid subscription key for authentication
    
- Different building types may require different input parameters
    
- Reverse calculators give you an indication of the maximum amount of energy or water your building can use to achieve your desired rating.
    

## References

This documentation is based on the NABERS Energy Rating Calculator API specifications.

# Office Tenancy Energy Reverse Rating Calculator

## Overview

This endpoint uses the NABERS reverse calculator to determine the energy consumption required to achieve a specific NABERS star rating for an Office Tenancy. The reverse calculator works by taking a target star rating and building characteristics as inputs, then calculating the required energy consumption to achieve that rating.

### Key Information

- **Purpose**: Calculate the energy consumption required to achieve a specific NABERS star rating
    
- **Authentication**: Requires an API subscription key in the header
    
- **Request Format**: JSON array containing building details and target rating
    
- **Response**: Returns the calculated energy consumption values needed to achieve the target rating
    

---

## Request Method and URL

- **Method:** POST
    
- **URL:** `https://data.nabers.gov.au/reversecalculator/calculate`
    

## Headers

| Key | Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key | `****` (masked) | API subscription key (required) |
| Authorization | Bearer | Bearer token for authentication (required) |

## Request Body

The request body is a JSON object containing a few sections:

1. Fields with basic information such as target star rating, postcode, premise type and rating type
    
2. premiseInputs object containing information about the premise attributes.
    
3. enrConsumptionInputs containing information about consumption for electricity, gas and diesel
    

### 1 Input Fields

| Field | Type | Description | Example |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| targetStar | string | Target star rating (enum) | "5.578" |
| premiseType | string | Type of premise (enum) | "Office" |
| ratingScope | string | Scope of rating | "Tenancy" |
| ratingType | string | Type of rating | "Energy" |
| premiseInputs | object | Inputs related to the premise | See nested table |
| enrConsumptionInputs | object | Energy consumption inputs | See nested table |

### 2 premiseInputs Object

| Field | Type | Description | Example |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| postcode | string | Postcode of the premise | "2000" |
| numberOfComputers | string | Number of computers | "513" |
| numberOfApartments | string or null | Number of apartments (nullable) | null |
| netLettableArea | string | Net lettable area in sqm | "610151" |
| hours | string | Operating hours per week | "48.5" |

### 3 enrConsumptionInputs Object

| Field | Type | Description | Example |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| lpgPercentage | string | Percentage of LPG consumption | "0" |
| gasPercentage | string | Percentage of gas consumption | "5.0" |
| electricityPercentage | string | Percentage of electricity consumption | "91.0" |
| dieselPercentage | string | Percentage of diesel consumption | "4" |

## Expected Response

``` json
[
  {
    "waterResults": null,
    "sequence": 1,
    "lstErrors": null,
    "energyResults": {
      "targetResidual": null,
      "targetMaxEnergyBF": null,
      "primaryIntermediateOutputs": null,
      "predictedNGE": "55",
      "predictedGE": 70.4717330357962530100913090454570400000000000000,
      "fuelConsumption": {
        "targetMaxLPG": null,
        "targetMaxGas": 29588,
        "targetMaxEnergyIntensity": 385,
        "targetMaxElec": 83102,
        "targetMaxDiesel": 0
      },
      "emissions": {
        "targetMaxEmissionsIntensity": 70.4717330357962530100913090454570400000000000000,
        "targetMaxEmissions": 60084.1995863198853164038500921566723040000000000000000000,
        "predictedAveEmissions": null,
        "actualTargetScope1_2_3": 60083,
        "actualTargetScope1_2": 56371
      }
    }
  }
]

 ```

## energyResults Object

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| targetResidual | null/number | Target residual value |
| targetMaxEnergyBF | null/number | Target maximum energy benchmark factor |
| primaryIntermediateOutputs | null/object | Primary intermediate outputs |
| predictedNGE | string | Predicted normalized greenhouse emissions |
| predictedGE | number | Predicted greenhouse emissions |
| fuelConsumption | object | Fuel consumption details |
| emissions | object | Emissions details |

## fuelConsumption Object

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| targetMaxLPG | null/number | Target maximum LPG consumption |
| targetMaxGas | number | Target maximum gas consumption |
| targetMaxEnergyIntensity | number | Target maximum energy intensity |
| targetMaxElec | number | Target maximum electricity consumption |
| targetMaxDiesel | number | Target maximum diesel consumption |

## emissions Object

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| targetMaxEmissionsIntensity | number | Target maximum emissions intensity |
| targetMaxEmissions | number | Target maximum emissions |
| predictedAveEmissions | null/number | Predicted average emissions |
| actualTargetScope1_2_3 | number | Actual target scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions |
| actualTargetScope1_2 | number | Actual target scope 1 and 2 emissions |

## Notes

- The API requires a valid subscription key for authentication
    
- Different building types may require different input parameters
    
- Reverse calculators give you an indication of the maximum amount of energy or water your building can use to achieve your desired rating.
    

## References

This documentation is based on the NABERS Energy Rating Calculator API specifications.

# Office Whole Building Water Reverse Rating Calculator

## Overview

This endpoint uses the NABERS reverse calculator to determine the water consumption required to achieve a specific NABERS star rating for an Office Whole Building. The reverse calculator works by taking a target star rating and building characteristics as inputs, then calculating the required water consumption to achieve that rating.

### Key Information

- **Purpose**: Calculate the water consumption required to achieve a specific NABERS star rating
    
- **Authentication**: Requires an API subscription key in the header
    
- **Request Format**: JSON array containing building details and target rating
    
- **Response**: Returns the calculated water consumption values needed to achieve the target rating
    

---

## Request Method and URL

- **Method:** POST
    
- **URL:** `https://data.nabers.gov.au/reversecalculator/calculate`
    

## Headers

| Key | Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key | `****` (masked) | API subscription key (required) |
| Authorization | Bearer | Bearer token for authentication (required) |

## Request Body

The request body is a JSON object containing a few sections:

1. Fields with basic information such as target star rating, postcode, premise type and rating type
    
2. premiseInputs object containing information about the premise attributes.
    

### 1 Input Fields

| Field | Type | Description | Example |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| targetStar | string | Target star rating (enum) | "5.12" |
| premiseType | string | Type of premise (enum) | "Office" |
| ratingScope | string | Scope of rating | "Whole Building" |
| ratingType | string | Type of rating | "Water" |
| premiseInputs | object | Inputs related to the premise | See below |
| enrConsumptionInputs | object/null | Energy consumption inputs (not required for Water rating) | null |

### 2 premiseInputs Object

| Field | Type | Description | Example |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| postcode | string | Postcode of the premise | "3000" |
| netLettableArea | string | Net lettable area in square meters | "759671.4" |
| hours | string | Operating hours per week | "55.0" |

## Expected Response

``` json
[
  {
    "waterResults": {
      "targetMaxWaterIntensity": "0.3290000000",
      "targetMaxWaterConsumption": 249931,
      "targetMaxWaterBF": null,
      "primaryIntermediateOutputs": null,
      "predictedAveWater": null
    },
    "sequence": 1,
    "lstErrors": null,
    "energyResults": null
  }
]

 ```

## Response

The response is an array of objects with the following structure:

| Field | Type | Description | Example |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| waterResults | object/null | Water related results (object or null) | See below |
| sequence | integer | Sequence number of the result | 1 |
| lstErrors | array/null | List of errors (null if none) | null |
| energyResults | object/null | Energy related results (null for Water) | null |

## waterResults Object

| Field | Type | Description | Example |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| targetMaxWaterIntensity | string | Maximum water intensity for target rating | "0.3290000000" |
| targetMaxWaterConsumption | number | Maximum water consumption in liters | 249931 |
| targetMaxWaterBF | null | Water benchmark factor | null |
| primaryIntermediateOutputs | null | Intermediate calculation outputs | null |
| predictedAveWater | null | Predicted average water usage | null |

## Notes

- The API requires a valid subscription key for authentication
    
- Different building types may require different input parameters
    
- Reverse calculators give you an indication of the maximum amount of energy or water your building can use to achieve your desired rating.
    

## References

This documentation is based on the NABERS Energy Rating Calculator API specifications.

### 2 premiseInputs Object

# Shopping Centre Water Reverse Rating Calculator

## Overview

This endpoint uses the NABERS reverse calculator to determine the water consumption required to achieve a specific NABERS star rating for a Shopping centre. The reverse calculator works by taking a target star rating and building characteristics as inputs, then calculating the required energy consumption to achieve that rating.

### Key Information

- **Purpose**: Calculate the energy consumption required to achieve a specific NABERS star rating
    
- **Authentication**: Requires an API subscription key in the header
    
- **Request Format**: JSON array containing building details and target rating
    
- **Response**: Returns the calculated energy consumption values needed to achieve the target rating
    

---

## Request Method and URL

- **Method:** POST
    
- **URL:** `https://data.nabers.gov.au/reversecalculator/calculate`
    

## Headers

| Key | Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key | `****` (masked) | API subscription key (required) |
| Authorization | Bearer | Bearer token for authentication (required) |

## Request Body

The request body is a JSON object containing a few sections:

1. Fields with basic information such as target star rating, postcode, premise type and rating type
    
2. premiseInputs object containing information about the premise attributes.
    

### 1 Input Fields

| Field | Type | Description | Example |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| targetStar | string | Target NABERS star rating | "5.71" |
| premiseType | string | Type of premise | "Shopping Centre" |
| ratingType | string | Type of rating | "Water" |
| premiseInputs | object | Premise details | (see below) |
| enrConsumptionInputs | object | Energy consumption breakdown (optional) | null |

---

### 2 premiseInputs Object

| Field | Type | Description | Example |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| postcode | string | Australian postcode | "3000" |
| gLAR | number | Gross Lettable Area Retail (m²) | 3000 |
| gLARServiced | number | GLA serviced by central plant (m²) | 750 |
| tradingDays | number | Trading days per year | 365 |
| hoursOfService | number | Hours of service per week | 50 |
| cPMechVentilated | number | Car park area mechanically ventilated (m²) | 0 |
| cPNaturallyVentilated | number | Car park area naturally ventilated (m²) | 0 |
| fcs | number | Food court seating (number of seats) | 100 |
| gLARgym | number | GLA serviced gym (m²) | 70 |
| theatrettes | number | Number of theatrettes | 2 |

---

---

## Example Response Body

``` json
[
  {
    "waterResults": {
      "targetMaxWaterIntensity": "0.5084581182000000000000",
      "targetMaxWaterConsumption": 1525,
      "targetMaxWaterBF": 34.185,
      "primaryIntermediateOutputs": null,
      "predictedAveWater": 4462.1160000000000000000
    },
    "sequence": 1,
    "lstErrors": null,
    "energyResults": null
  }
]

 ```

---

## waterResults Object

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| targetMaxWaterIntensity | string | The maximum water intensity (kL/m²) to achieve the target star rating |
| targetMaxWaterConsumption | number | The maximum water consumption (kL) to achieve the target star rating |
| targetMaxWaterBF | number | The maximum water benchmark factor to achieve the target star rating |
| primaryIntermediateOutputs | object | Additional calculation outputs (null for water ratings) |
| predictedAveWater | number | The predicted average water consumption for the building type |

## Notes

- The API requires a valid subscription key for authentication
    
- Different building types may require different input parameters
    
- Reverse calculators give you an indication of the maximum amount of energy or water your building can use to achieve your desired rating.

# Hotel Energy Reverse Rating Calculator

## Overview

This endpoint uses the NABERS reverse calculator to determine the energy consumption required to achieve a specific NABERS star rating for a Hotel building. The reverse calculator works by taking a target star rating and building characteristics as inputs, then calculating the required energy consumption to achieve that rating.

### Key Information

- **Purpose**: Calculate the energy consumption required to achieve a specific NABERS star rating
    
- **Authentication**: Requires an API subscription key in the header
    
- **Request Format**: JSON array containing building details and target rating
    
- **Response**: Returns the calculated energy consumption values needed to achieve the target rating
    

---

## Request Method and URL

- **Method:** POST
    
- **URL:** `https://data.nabers.gov.au/reversecalculator/calculate`
    

## Headers

| Key | Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key | `****` (masked) | API subscription key (required) |
| Authorization | Bearer | Bearer token for authentication (required) |

## Request Body

The request body is a JSON object containing a few sections:

1. Fields with basic information such as target star rating, postcode, premise type and rating type
    
2. premiseInputs object containing information about the premise attributes.
    
3. enrConsumptionInputs containing information about consumption for electricity, gas and diesel
    

### 1 Input Fields

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| targetStar | string | Yes | The target NABERS star rating to achieve (e.g., "5.15", "4.5") |
| premiseType | string | Yes | Type of building - must be "Hotel" for this endpoint |
| ratingType | string | Yes | Type of rating - must be "Energy" for this endpoint |

### 2 premiseInputs Object

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| postcode | string | Yes | Australian postcode of the hotel location |
| aAAStar | string | Yes | Hotel's AAA star rating (e.g., "4", "5") |
| rooms | number | Yes | Number of rooms in the hotel |
| nFsl | number | Yes | Number of full-time equivalent staff |
| nFuncSeats | number | Yes | Number of function seats |
| aHeatedPool | number | Yes | Area of heated pool in square meters (0 if none) |

### 3 enrConsumptionInputs Object

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| electricityPercentage | number | Yes | Percentage of electricity in the energy mix |
| gasPercentage | number | Yes | Percentage of gas in the energy mix |
| dieselPercentage | number | Yes | Percentage of diesel in the energy mix |

## Response Structure

The response contains calculated energy consumption values required to achieve the target NABERS star rating.

### energyResults Object

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| targetResidual | number | The residual value for the target rating calculation |
| targetMaxEnergyBF | null/number | Maximum energy benchmark figure for the target rating |
| primaryIntermediateOutputs | null/object | Intermediate calculation outputs |
| predictedNGE | null/number | Predicted normalized greenhouse emissions |
| predictedGE | null/number | Predicted greenhouse emissions |

### fuelConsumption Object

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| targetMaxLPG | null/number | Maximum LPG consumption (in MJ) to achieve target rating |
| targetMaxGas | number | Maximum gas consumption (in MJ) to achieve target rating |
| targetMaxEnergyIntensity | number | Maximum energy intensity (in MJ/m²) to achieve target rating |
| targetMaxElec | number | Maximum electricity consumption (in kWh) to achieve target rating |
| targetMaxDiesel | number | Maximum diesel consumption (in L) to achieve target rating |

### emissions Object

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| targetMaxEmissionsIntensity | number | Maximum emissions intensity (in kg CO₂-e/m²) to achieve target rating |
| targetMaxEmissions | number | Maximum total emissions (in kg CO₂-e) to achieve target rating |
| predictedAveEmissions | number | Predicted average emissions (in kg CO₂-e) |
| actualTargetScope1_2_3 | number | Target emissions for scopes 1, 2, and 3 (in kg CO₂-e) |
| actualTargetScope1_2 | number | Target emissions for scopes 1 and 2 (in kg CO₂-e) |

## Notes

- The API requires a valid subscription key for authentication
    
- Different building types may require different input parameters
    
- Reverse calculators give you an indication of the maximum amount of energy or water your building can use to achieve your desired rating.
    

## References

This documentation is based on the NABERS Energy Rating Calculator API specifications.

request.description:
# Apartment Building Energy Reverse Rating Calculator

## Overview

This endpoint uses the NABERS reverse calculator to determine the energy consumption required to achieve a specific NABERS star rating for an Apartment building. The reverse calculator works by taking a target star rating and building characteristics as inputs, then calculating the required energy consumption to achieve that rating.

### Key Information

- **Purpose**: Calculate the energy consumption required to achieve a specific NABERS star rating
    
- **Authentication**: Requires an API subscription key in the header
    
- **Request Format**: JSON array containing building details and target rating
    
- **Response**: Returns the calculated energy consumption values needed to achieve the target rating
    

---

## Request Method and URL

- **Method:** POST
    
- **URL:** `https://data.nabers.gov.au/reversecalculator/calculate`
    

## Headers

| Key | Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key | `****` (masked) | API subscription key (required) |
| Authorization | Bearer | Bearer token for authentication (required) |

## Request Body

The request body is a JSON object containing a few sections:

1. Fields with basic information such as target star rating, postcode, premise type and rating type
    
2. premiseInputs object containing information about the premise attributes.
    
3. enrConsumptionInputs containing information about consumption for electricity, gas and diesel
    

### 1 Input Fields

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| targetStar | string | The target NABERS star rating to achieve (e.g., "6.5", "4.5") |
| premiseType | string | Type of building - must be "Apartment Building" for this endpoint |
| ratingType | string | Type of rating - must be "Energy" for this endpoint |

### 2 premiseInputs Object

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| postcode | string | Australian postcode of the building location |
| numberOfApartments | number | Total number of apartments in the building |
| nCAC | number | Net common area conditioned (in square meters) |
| nCW | number | Net common area unconditioned (in square meters) |
| nLift | number | Number of lifts in the building |
| pool | string | Type of pool ("Temperature controlled pool", "Non-temperature controlled pool", or "No pool") |
| tMPA | number | Total months pool is available |
| gym | string | Whether the building has a gym ("yes" or "no") |
| tMGA | number | Total months gym is available |
| cPMechVentilated | string | Car park mechanically ventilated area (in square meters) |
| cPNaturallyVentilated | string | Car park naturally ventilated area (in square meters) |

### 3 enrConsumptionInputs Object

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| electricityPercentage | number | Yes | Percentage of energy consumption from electricity (0-100) |
| gasPercentage | number | Yes | Percentage of energy consumption from gas (0-100) |
| dieselPercentage | number | Yes | Percentage of energy consumption from diesel (0-100) |

## Expected Response

``` json
[
  {
    "waterResults": null,
    "sequence": 1,
    "lstErrors": null,
    "energyResults": {
      "targetResidual": null,
      "targetMaxEnergyBF": 13.255,
      "primaryIntermediateOutputs": null,
      "predictedNGE": null,
      "predictedGE": null,
      "fuelConsumption": {
        "targetMaxLPG": null,
        "targetMaxGas": 1051413,
        "targetMaxEnergyIntensity": 3504,
        "targetMaxElec": 584118,
        "targetMaxDiesel": 9079
      },
      "emissions": {
        "targetMaxEmissionsIntensity": 567,
        "targetMaxEmissions": 567238,
        "predictedAveEmissions": 4279430,
        "actualTargetScope1_2_3": 567231,
        "actualTargetScope1_2": 493504
      }
    }
  }
]

 ```

## energyResults Object

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| targetMaxEnergyBF | number | Target maximum energy benchmark factor |
| targetResidual | number | Target residual value |
| primaryIntermediateOutputs | object | Primary intermediate outputs |
| predictedNGE | number | Predicted net greenhouse emissions |
| predictedGE | number | Predicted gross emissions |

## fuelConsumption Object

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| targetMaxElec | number | Target maximum electricity consumption (kWh) |
| targetMaxGas | number | Target maximum gas consumption (MJ) |
| targetMaxDiesel | number | Target maximum diesel consumption (L) |
| targetMaxLPG | number | Target maximum LPG consumption |
| targetMaxEnergyIntensity | number | Target maximum energy intensity (MJ/m²) |

## emissions Object

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| targetMaxEmissions | number | Target maximum emissions (kg CO₂-e) |
| targetMaxEmissionsIntensity | number | Target maximum emissions intensity (kg CO₂-e/m²) |
| predictedAveEmissions | number | Predicted average emissions (kg CO₂-e) |
| actualTargetScope1_2 | number | Actual target scope 1 and 2 emissions (kg CO₂-e) |
| actualTargetScope1_2_3 | number | Actual target scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions (kg CO₂-e) |

## Notes

- The API requires a valid subscription key for authentication
    
- Different building types may require different input parameters
    
- Reverse calculators give you an indication of the maximum amount of energy or water your building can use to achieve your desired rating.
    

## References

This documentation is based on the NABERS Energy Rating Calculator API specifications.

``` json
            "primaryIntermediateOutputs": null,
 ```

``` json
            }

 ```

---

### energyResults Object

The `energyResults` object contains the following properties:

| Property | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| starRating | string | The target NABERS star rating |
| totalEnergy | number | Total energy consumption in MJ/m²/year |
| benchmark | number | Benchmark energy consumption for the given star rating |
| percentile | number | Percentile ranking compared to similar buildings |

---

### fuelConsumption Object

The `fuelConsumption` object contains the following properties:

| Property | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| electricity | number | Electricity consumption in MJ/m²/year |
| gas | number | Gas consumption in MJ/m²/year |
| lpg | number | LPG consumption in MJ/m²/year |
| diesel | number | Diesel consumption in MJ/m²/year |

---

### emissions Object

The `emissions` object contains the following properties:

| Property | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| totalEmissions | number | Total greenhouse gas emissions in kg CO₂-e/m²/year |
| electricityEmissions | number | Emissions from electricity in kg CO₂-e/m²/year |
| gasEmissions | number | Emissions from gas in kg CO₂-e/m²/year |
| lpgEmissions | number | Emissions from LPG in kg CO₂-e/m²/year |
| dieselEmissions | number | Emissions from diesel in kg CO₂-e/m²/year |

## Notes

- The API requires a valid subscription key for authentication
    
- Different building types may require different input parameters
    
- Reverse calculators give you an indication of the maximum amount of energy or water your building can use to achieve your desired rating.

This documentation is based on the NABERS Energy Rating Calculator API specifications.

request.description:
# RACRL Retirement Living Water Reverse Calculator

## Overview

This endpoint uses the NABERS reverse calculator to determine the water consumption required to achieve a specific NABERS star rating for a RACRL Retirement Living building. The reverse calculator works by taking a target star rating and building characteristics as inputs, then calculating the required energy consumption to achieve that rating.

### Key Information

- **Purpose**: Calculate the energy consumption required to achieve a specific NABERS star rating
    
- **Authentication**: Requires an API subscription key in the header
    
- **Request Format**: JSON array containing building details and target rating
    
- **Response**: Returns the calculated energy consumption values needed to achieve the target rating
    

---

## Request Method and URL

- **Method:** POST
    
- **URL:** `https://data.nabers.gov.au/reversecalculator/calculate`
    

## Headers

| Key | Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key | `****` (masked) | API subscription key (required) |
| Authorization | Bearer | Bearer token for authentication (required) |

## Request Body

The request body is a JSON object containing a few sections:

1. Fields with basic information such as target star rating, postcode, premise type and rating type
    
2. premiseInputs object containing information about the premise attributes.
    

### 1 Input Fields

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| targetStar | string | Yes | The target NABERS star rating to achieve (e.g., "5.15", "4.5") |
| premiseType | string | Yes | Type of building - must be "Hotel" for this endpoint |
| ratingType | string | Yes | Type of rating - must be "Energy" for this endpoint |

## 2 premiseInputs Object

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| premiseName | string | Yes | Name of the retirement living facility |
| streetAddress | string | Yes | Street address of the facility |
| suburb | string | Yes | Suburb where the facility is located |
| state | string | Yes | State where the facility is located (e.g., NSW, VIC, QLD) |
| postcode | string | Yes | Postal code of the facility location |
| ratingPeriodStartDate | string | Yes | Start date of the rating period in ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD) |
| ratingPeriodEndDate | string | Yes | End date of the rating period in ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD) |
| numberOfUnits | number | Yes | Total number of independent living units in the facility |
| numberOfBedrooms | number | Yes | Total number of bedrooms across all units |
| numberOfOccupiedUnits | number | Yes | Number of occupied units during the rating period |
| hasPool | boolean | Yes | Indicates if the facility has a swimming pool |
| poolArea | number | Conditional | Required if hasPool is true; area of the pool in square meters |
| hasCarWashingFacilities | boolean | Yes | Indicates if the facility has car washing facilities |
| hasCommunalLaundry | boolean | Yes | Indicates if the facility has communal laundry facilities |
| irrigatedGardenArea | number | Yes | Area of irrigated gardens in square meters |

## Expected Response

``` json
{
        "waterResults": {
            "targetMaxWaterIntensity": "53",
            "targetMaxWaterConsumption": 10766,
            "targetMaxWaterBF": 1.28533896853047004397738306014050,
            "primaryIntermediateOutputs": null,
            "predictedAveWater": 8376
        },
        "sequence": 1,
        "lstErrors": null,
        "energyResults": null
    }

 ```

## waterResults Object

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| starRating | number | The calculated NABERS star rating (0-6 stars, can include half stars) |
| waterIndex | number | The water performance index (ratio of current to benchmark consumption) |
| benchmarkWaterConsumption | number | The benchmark water consumption for a facility of this type and size (kL) |
| currentWaterConsumption | number | The actual water consumption during the rating period (kL) |
| normalizedWaterConsumption | number | The normalized water consumption adjusted for variables like occupancy (kL) |
| waterSavings | number | The difference between benchmark and current water consumption (kL) |

## Notes

- The API requires a valid subscription key for authentication
    
- Different building types may require different input parameters
    
- Reverse calculators give you an indication of the maximum amount of energy or water your building can use to achieve your desired rating.
    

## References

This documentation is based on the NABERS Energy Rating Calculator API specifications.

request.description:
# RACRL Residential Aged Care Energy Reverse Rating Calculator

## Overview

This endpoint uses the NABERS reverse calculator to determine the energy consumption required to achieve a specific NABERS star rating for a RACRL Residential Aged Care building. The reverse calculator works by taking a target star rating and building characteristics as inputs, then calculating the required energy consumption to achieve that rating.

### Key Information

- **Purpose**: Calculate the energy consumption required to achieve a specific NABERS star rating
    
- **Authentication**: Requires an API subscription key in the header
    
- **Request Format**: JSON array containing building details and target rating
    
- **Response**: Returns the calculated energy consumption values needed to achieve the target rating
    

---

## Request Method and URL

- **Method:** POST
    
- **URL:** `https://data.nabers.gov.au/reversecalculator/calculate`
    

## Headers

| Key | Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key | `****` (masked) | API subscription key (required) |
| Authorization | Bearer | Bearer token for authentication (required) |

## Request Body

The request body is a JSON object containing a few sections:

1. Fields with basic information such as target star rating, postcode, premise type and rating type
    
2. premiseInputs object containing information about the premise attributes.
    
3. enrConsumptionInputs containing information about consumption for electricity, gas and diesel
    

### 1 Input Fields

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| targetStar | string | Target NABERS star rating (e.g., "6.4") |
| premiseType | string | Type of premise. For this request, use "RACRL" |
| ratingScope | string | Scope of rating. For residential aged care, use "Residential Aged Care" |
| ratingType | string | Type of rating. Use "Energy" |

---

### 2 premiseInputs Object

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| postcode | string | Yes | Australian postcode of the facility location |
| area | number | Yes | Total area of the facility in square meters (0 if not specified) |
| obd | number | Yes | Occupied bed days - total number of days beds were occupied during the rating period |
| heatedPool | string | Optional | Whether the facility has a heated pool ("yes" or "no") |
| heatedPoolArea | number | Optional | Area of the heated pool in square meters (required if heatedPool is "yes") |
| heavyLaundry | string | Optional | Whether the facility has heavy laundry facilities ("yes" or "no") |
| mealsNonResident | number | Optional | Number of meals served to non-residents during the rating period |

## 3 enrConsumptionInputs Object

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| electricityPercentage | number | Yes | Percentage of electricity usage (0-100) |
| gasPercentage | number | Yes | Percentage of gas usage (0-100) |
| lpgPercentage | number | Yes | Percentage of LPG usage (0-100) |
| dieselPercentage | number | Yes | Percentage of diesel usage (0-100) |

## Expected Response

``` json
[
  {
    "waterResults": null,
    "sequence": 1,
    "lstErrors": null,
    "energyResults": {
      "targetResidual": -0.5123456789,
      "targetMaxEnergyBF": null,
      "primaryIntermediateOutputs": null,
      "predictedNGE": null,
      "predictedGE": null,
      "fuelConsumption": {
        "targetMaxLPG": 12345,
        "targetMaxGas": 987654,
        "targetMaxEnergyIntensity": 32500,
        "targetMaxElec": 1234567,
        "targetMaxDiesel": 12345
      },
      "emissions": {
        "targetMaxEmissionsIntensity": 6500,
        "targetMaxEmissions": 1500000,
        "predictedAveEmissions": 3200000,
        "actualTargetScope1_2_3": 1500000,
        "actualTargetScope1_2": 1350000
      }
    }
  }
]

 ```

## energyResults Object

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| targetResidual | number | The residual value for the target calculation |
| targetMaxEnergyBF | null | Not used in this calculation |
| primaryIntermediateOutputs | null | Not used in this calculation |
| predictedNGE | null | Not used in this calculation |
| predictedGE | null | Not used in this calculation |

## fuelConsumption Object

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| targetMaxLPG | number | Maximum LPG consumption (MJ/year) to achieve the target rating |
| targetMaxGas | number | Maximum gas consumption (MJ/year) to achieve the target rating |
| targetMaxEnergyIntensity | number | Maximum energy intensity (MJ/bed-day) to achieve the target rating |
| targetMaxElec | number | Maximum electricity consumption (kWh/year) to achieve the target rating |
| targetMaxDiesel | number | Maximum diesel consumption (L/year) to achieve the target rating |

## emissions Object

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| targetMaxEmissionsIntensity | number | Maximum emissions intensity (kg CO₂-e/bed-day) to achieve the target rating |
| targetMaxEmissions | number | Maximum total emissions (kg CO₂-e/year) to achieve the target rating |
| predictedAveEmissions | number | Predicted average emissions for similar facilities |
| actualTargetScope1_2_3 | number | Target emissions for Scope 1, 2, and 3 (kg CO₂-e/year) |
| actualTargetScope1_2 | number | Target emissions for Scope 1 and 2 (kg CO₂-e/year) |

## Notes

- The API requires a valid subscription key for authentication
    
- Different building types may require different input parameters
    
- Reverse calculators give you an indication of the maximum amount of energy or water your building can use to achieve your desired rating.
    

## References

This documentation is based on the NABERS Energy Rating Calculator API specifications.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |

| postcode | string | Yes | Australian postcode where the facility is located |

- Different building types may require different input parameters
    
- Reverse calculators give you an indication of the maximum amount of energy or water your building can use to achieve your desired rating.
    

## References

- Different building types may require different input parameters
    
- Reverse calculators give you an indication of the maximum amount of energy or water your building can use to achieve your desired rating.
    

## References

request.description:
# Warehouse and Cold Stores Energy Reverse Rating Calculator

## Overview

This endpoint uses the NABERS reverse calculator to determine the energy consumption required to achieve a specific NABERS star rating for a Warehouse and Cold Stores Facility. The reverse calculator works by taking a target star rating and building characteristics as inputs, then calculating the required energy consumption to achieve that rating.

### Key Information

- **Purpose**: Calculate the energy consumption required to achieve a specific NABERS star rating
    
- **Authentication**: Requires an API subscription key in the header
    
- **Request Format**: JSON array containing building details and target rating
    
- **Response**: Returns the calculated energy consumption values needed to achieve the target rating
    

---

## Request Method and URL

- **Method:** POST
    
- **URL:** `https://data.nabers.gov.au/reversecalculator/calculate`
    

## Headers

| Key | Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key | `****` (masked) | API subscription key (required) |
| Authorization | Bearer | Bearer token for authentication (required) |

## Request Body

The request body is a JSON object containing a few sections:

1. Fields with basic information such as target star rating, postcode, premise type and rating type
    
2. premiseInputs object containing information about the premise attributes.
    
3. enrConsumptionInputs containing information about consumption for electricity, gas and diesel
    

### 1 Input Fields

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| targetStar | string | Target NABERS star rating (e.g., "4.5") |
| premiseType | string | Type of premise. For this request, use "Warehouse and cold store" |
| ratingType | string | Type of rating. Use "Energy" |

---

### 2 premiseInputs Object

| Property | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| postcode | string | Premise postcode (e.g., "2000") |
| area | string/number | Total area in square meters |
| operationalData | string | Type of operational data used (e.g., "FTE" for Full-Time Employees) |
| coldstorePresence | boolean | Whether the facility has cold storage (true or false) |
| totalGLA | number | Total Gross Leasable Area in square meters |
| conditionedGLA | number | Conditioned Gross Leasable Area in square meters |
| nonconditionedGLA | number | Non-conditioned Gross Leasable Area in square meters |
| operatingHours | number | Operating hours per week |
| coldRoomVolume | number | Volume of cold room storage in cubic meters |
| coolRoomVolume | number | Volume of cool room storage in cubic meters |
| annualTurnoverRatio | number | Annual turnover ratio |
| fullTimeEmployees | number | Number of full-time employees |

---

### 3 enrConsumptionInputs Object

| Property | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| electricityPercentage | string/number | Percentage of electricity consumption (e.g., "70") |
| gasPercentage | string/number | Percentage of gas consumption (e.g., "10") |
| lpgPercentage | string/number | Percentage of LPG consumption (e.g., "10") |
| dieselPercentage | string/number | Percentage of diesel consumption (e.g., "10") |

---

## Expected Response

``` json
 {
        "waterResults": null,
        "sequence": 1,
        "lstErrors": null,
        "energyResults": {
            "targetResidual": null,
            "targetMaxEnergyBF": null,
            "primaryIntermediateOutputs": null,
            "predictedNGE": null,
            "predictedGE": null,
            "fuelConsumption": {
                "targetMaxLPG": 16905,
                "targetMaxGas": 434464,
                "targetMaxEnergyIntensity": 9654.75647292885828792194550478762,
                "targetMaxElec": 844791,
                "targetMaxDiesel": 11255
            },
            "emissions": {
                "targetMaxEmissionsIntensity": 1523,
                "targetMaxEmissions": 685533.88732769070336000000000000034276694366384535168000000,
                "predictedAveEmissions": 1169853.05004725376000000,
                "actualTargetScope1_2_3": 692551.117620000000,
                "actualTargetScope1_2": 636776.073620000000
            }
        }
    }

 ```

## Response

| Section | Description |
| --- | --- |
| energyResults | Contains the star rating and energy consumption details |
| fuelConsumption | Breakdown of energy consumption by fuel type |
| emissions | Breakdown of emissions by fuel type |

---

### energyResults Object

| Property | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| starRating | string | The target NABERS star rating |
| totalEnergy | number | Total energy consumption in MJ/m²/year |
| benchmark | number | Benchmark energy consumption for the given star rating |
| percentile | number | Percentile ranking compared to similar buildings |

---

### fuelConsumption Object

| Property | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| electricity | number | Electricity consumption in MJ/m²/year |
| gas | number | Gas consumption in MJ/m²/year |
| lpg | number | LPG consumption in MJ/m²/year |
| diesel | number | Diesel consumption in MJ/m²/year |

---

### emissions Object

| Property | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| totalEmissions | number | Total greenhouse gas emissions in kg CO₂-e/m²/year |
| electricityEmissions | number | Emissions from electricity in kg CO₂-e/m²/year |
| gasEmissions | number | Emissions from gas in kg CO₂-e/m²/year |
| lpgEmissions | number | Emissions from LPG in kg CO₂-e/m²/year |
| dieselEmissions | number | Emissions from diesel in kg CO₂-e/m²/year |

## Notes

- The API requires a valid subscription key for authentication
    
- Different building types may require different input parameters
    
- Reverse calculators give you an indication of the maximum amount of energy or water your building can use to achieve your desired rating.
    

## References

This documentation is based on the NABERS Energy Rating Calculator API specifications.

# Office Base Building Energy Reverse Rating Calculator

## Overview

This endpoint uses the NABERS reverse calculator to determine the energy consumption required to achieve a specific NABERS star rating for an Office Base Building. The reverse calculator works by taking a target star rating and building characteristics as inputs, then calculating the required energy consumption to achieve that rating.

### Key Information

- **Purpose**: Calculate the energy consumption required to achieve a specific NABERS star rating
    
- **Authentication**: Requires an API subscription key in the header
    
- **Request Format**: JSON array containing building details and target rating
    
- **Response**: Returns the calculated energy consumption values needed to achieve the target rating
    

---

## Request Method and URL

- **Method:** POST
    
- **URL:** `https://data.nabers.gov.au/reversecalculator/calculate`
    

## Headers

| Key | Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key | `****` (masked) | API subscription key (required) |
| Authorization | Bearer | Bearer token for authentication (required) |

## Request Body

The request body is a JSON object containing a few sections:

1. Fields with basic information such as target star rating, postcode, premise type and rating type
    
2. premiseInputs object containing information about the premise attributes.
    
3. enrConsumptionInputs containing information about consumption for electricity, gas and diesel
    

### 1 Input Fields

| Field | Type | Description | Example Value |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| targetStar | string | Target NABERS star rating (enum) | "5.7" |
| premiseType | string | Type of premise (enum) | "Office" |
| ratingScope | string | Scope of rating | "Base Building" |
| ratingType | string | Type of rating | "Energy" |
| premiseInputs | object | Inputs related to the premise | See nested table below |
| enrConsumptionInputs | object | Energy consumption inputs | See nested table below |

### 2 premiseInputs Object

| Field | Type | Description | Example Value |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| postcode | string | Postcode of the premise | "2615" |
| numberOfComputers | string | Number of computers (decimal) | "0.000000" |
| netLettableArea | string | Net lettable area in square meters | "10888.00" |
| hours | string | Operating hours per week | "40" |

### 3 enrConsumptionInputs Object

| Field | Type | Description | Example Value |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| electricityPercentage | string | Percentage of electricity usage | "90" |
| gasPercentage | string | Percentage of gas usage | "7" |
| dieselPercentage | string | Percentage of diesel usage | "3" |

---

## Expected Response

``` json
[
  {
    "targetStar": "5.7",
    "premiseType": "Office",
    "ratingScope": "Base Building",
    "ratingType": "Energy",
    "premiseInputs": {
      "postcode": "2615",
      "numberOfComputers": "0.000000",
      "netLettableArea": "10888.00",
      "hours": "40"
    },
    "enrConsumptionInputs": {
      "electricityPercentage": "90",
      "gasPercentage": "7",
      "dieselPercentage": "3"
    },
    "energyResults": {
      "energyConsumption": "1,151,000.00",
      "energyConsumptionPerM2": "105.71",
      "energyConsumptionPerM2Benchmark": "105.71",
      "energyConsumptionPerM2BenchmarkNormalized": "105.71",
      "energyConsumptionPerM2Normalized": "105.71",
      "energyConsumptionNormalized": "1,151,000.00",
      "starRating": "5.7",
      "starRatingNormalized": "5.7"
    },
    "fuelConsumption": {
      "electricity": "1,035,900.00",
      "gas": "80,570.00",
      "diesel": "34,530.00"
    },
    "emissions": {
      "emissionsConsumption": "1,035,900.00",
      "emissionsConsumptionPerM2": "95.14",
      "emissionsConsumptionPerM2Benchmark": "95.14",
      "emissionsConsumptionPerM2BenchmarkNormalized": "95.14",
      "emissionsConsumptionPerM2Normalized": "95.14",
      "emissionsConsumptionNormalized": "1,035,900.00"
    }
  }
]

 ```

## energyResults Object

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| energyConsumption | string | Total energy consumption (MJ) |
| energyConsumptionPerM2 | string | Energy consumption per square meter (MJ/m²) |
| energyConsumptionPerM2Benchmark | string | Benchmark energy consumption per square meter (MJ/m²) |
| energyConsumptionPerM2BenchmarkNormalized | string | Normalized benchmark energy consumption per square meter (MJ/m²) |
| energyConsumptionPerM2Normalized | string | Normalized energy consumption per square meter (MJ/m²) |
| energyConsumptionNormalized | string | Normalized total energy consumption (MJ) |
| starRating | string | Calculated NABERS star rating |
| starRatingNormalized | string | Normalized NABERS star rating |

## fuelConsumption Object

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| electricity | string | Electricity consumption (MJ) |
| gas | string | Gas consumption (MJ) |
| diesel | string | Diesel consumption (MJ) |

## emissions Object

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| emissionsConsumption | string | Total emissions (kg CO₂-e) |
| emissionsConsumptionPerM2 | string | Emissions per square meter (kg CO₂-e/m²) |
| emissionsConsumptionPerM2Benchmark | string | Benchmark emissions per square meter (kg CO₂-e/m²) |
| emissionsConsumptionPerM2BenchmarkNormalized | string | Normalized benchmark emissions per square meter (kg CO₂-e/m²) |
| emissionsConsumptionPerM2Normalized | string | Normalized emissions per square meter (kg CO₂-e/m²) |
| emissionsConsumptionNormalized | string | Normalized total emissions (kg CO₂-e) |

## Notes

- The API requires a valid subscription key for authentication
    
- Different building types may require different input parameters
    
- Reverse calculators give you an indication of the maximum amount of energy or water your building can use to achieve your desired rating.
    

## References

This documentation is based on the NABERS Energy Rating Calculator API specifications.

item.description:
## What is Reverse Rating Calculator API? The Reverse Rating calculators give you an indication of the maximum amount of energy and water your building can use to achieve your desired rating. Using the API would allow the customer to programmatically calculate the data directly into their own built applications. The NABERS Reverse Rating Calculator API is an API that the customer can call to retrieve the NABERS algorithm based on specific parameters that will be included in each call. The Reverse Rating Calculator API is available for the following sectors, scopes and rating types | **Building Type** | **Building Scope** | **Subsector** | **Rating Type** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Office | Base Building
Whole Building
Tenancy | | Energy
Water
Inddor Environment | | Shopping centres | Not applicable | | Energy
Water | | Hotel | Not applicable | | Energy
Water | | Data Centre | Infrastructure
IT equipment
Whole Facility | | Energy | | Apartment Building | Not applicable | | Energy
Water | | Residental Aged Care Retirement Living (RACRL) | Residential Aged Care
Retirement Living
Co-Located | | Energy
Water | | Warehouse and Cold Stores | Not applicable | | Energy | ## Bulk Requests The Rating reverse calculator supports bulks requests in a single call to the calculator. This feature can be useful for customers who need to make multiple calls to the calculator based on different input parameters. For example, a customer may like to send three different requests for an office building where the computer count, area and consumption inputs differ in each request. The calculator would then return three different output with different calculation for every request sent. The recommended number of requests in a single call is 100 for optimal performance. An example of a bulk call is provided in the folder, it is labelled "Bulk reverse calculator request example for Offices" ## API Call Limits Currently there are both daily and minute limits than can be made to the Rating Register API per subscription. These are indicated in the table below. | **Type of Limit** | **Amount** | | --- | --- | | Daily limit | 1000 calls per day | | Calls per minute | 200 calls per minute | | Bulk request | 100 calculations in a single request | ## Real-time Data The API calls directly the calculator API and results are returned in real-time ## Potential Errors Below is a list of some errors you might receive when calling the Rating Calculator API | **Error Code** | **Error Message** | **Possible reasons** | | --- | --- | --- | | 401 | Access denied due to invalid subscription key. Make sure to provide a valid key for an active subscription. | Please check that a valid API subscription key is entered in the header of the request. | | 404 | Resource not found | Please check the URL syntax for errors | | | The climate zone for this postcode 27500 cannot be found. Please contact NABERS for support.
The gross emission factor for this postcode 27500 cannot be found. Please contact NABERS for support.
The NGA emission factor for this postcode 27500 cannot be found. Please contact NABERS for support. | The postCode used in the request is invalid. Please verify the value entered. | | N/A | Invalid premiseType value | The entered premiseType is wrong. Please verify the value entered. You can reference the sample request to find the right syntax | | N/A | Invalid ratingScope value | The entered ratingScope is wrong. Please verify the value entered. You can reference the sample request to find the right syntax | | N/A | Malformed JSON: Expected '{' at the beginning of object | If the request structure is wrong then this message is shown. Check the input request and make sure it has all rating input parameters like control, ratinginputs and waterinputs and the values entered against each input request variable is correct. You can reference the sample request to find the right syntax | | N/A | Rating cannot be more than 6.9 targetStars | The star rating value should be a value between 0 and 6.9 | The sample requests in this folder are meant to guide our customers on how to structure their API calls. If you are still facing issue using the Rating Reverse Calculator API then please reach out to NABERS team.
